services.title: OUR SERVICES
services.description: HACO Logistics is not just a transportation company — we are a solution. We understand the challenges in global supply chains and are committed to supporting you with professional and flexible international shipping services, from sea freight and air freight to road transport. HACO Logistics helps you optimize your business operations, minimize risks, and enhance your competitive advantage.

sea.title: Sea Freight
sea.description: A cost-effective solution for large volume shipments. We connect you to a network of major ports worldwide, ensuring your cargo is transported safely, on schedule, and at optimal cost.

air.title: Air Freight
air.description: When speed is the key factor, our air freight service is the perfect choice. HACO Logistics ensures your cargo is transported quickly and safely to any destination in the world.

road.title: Road Transport
road.description: To complete the supply chain, we provide comprehensive domestic road transport services, ensuring goods are delivered door-to-door from seaports and airports to your warehouse quickly and safely.

customs.title: Customs Declaration
customs.description: We simplify all complex procedures. Our team of experts will help you complete customs declaration processes quickly and accurately, ensuring smooth customs clearance and avoiding unnecessary risks and delays.

items.question: What Documents Are Required for Sea Export Procedures?
items.answer:
The specific documents required for sea export procedures may vary depending on the destination country, type of goods being exported, and local regulations. However, common documents include:

- Commercial invoice
- Packing list
- Bill of Lading
- Certificate of Origin
- Export license (if required)
- Phytosanitary certificate (for agricultural products)
- Other certificates or permits as required by the importing country

items.question: What Documents Are Required for Air Export Procedures?
items.answer:
The specific documents required for air export procedures may vary depending on the destination country, type of goods being exported, and local regulations. However, common documents include:

- Commercial invoice
- Packing list
- Airway Bill (AWB)
- Certificate of Origin
- Export license
- Other certificates or permits: Depending on cargo type and destination country, additional documents may be required, such as a health certificate for food products or a safety certificate for hazardous materials.

items.question: What Documents Are Required for Sea Import Procedures?
items.answer:
The specific documents required for sea import procedures may vary depending on the country of origin, cargo type, and local regulations. However, common documents include:

- Commercial invoice
- Packing list
- Bill of Lading
- Import permit or license
- Customs declaration
- Certificate of Origin
- Other certificates or permits

items.question: What Documents Are Needed to Apply for an Import/Export License?
items.answer:
CONDITIONS

1. First: Import/export goods must have a permit. The law requires that permit-controlled goods must be licensed by the relevant Ministry. In addition, your goods must not be in the prohibited or temporarily suspended import/export list.

2. Second: Applicant eligibility. Entities that may apply for import permits include:
- Vietnamese enterprises without foreign-invested capital from foreign companies or organizations.
- Foreign-invested enterprises, branches, or foreign companies in Vietnam must comply with Vietnam's import/export regulations and the roadmap announced by the Ministry of Industry and Trade when applying for permits.

IMPORT/EXPORT LICENSE APPLICATION DOSSIER includes:

- Business registration certificate
- Certificate of origin for exported/imported goods
- Commercial invoices related to product transactions
- Freight invoice
- Payment confirmation
- Commercial contracts for the supply of goods between parties in a transaction

items.question: What Is Commercial Cargo?
items.answer:
Commercial cargo refers to goods bought and sold for business purposes, with economic value and circulation in the market.

Examples:

- Manufactured products: electronics, garments, furniture, automobiles
- Raw materials: minerals, agricultural products, timber
- Semi-finished goods: components, parts
- Food and beverages
- Machinery and equipment

items.question: What Is Non-Commercial Cargo?
items.answer:
Non-commercial cargo refers to goods not intended for sale or exchange. They are usually imported or exported for personal use, gifts, or other non-profit purposes.

Examples:

- Personal belongings: clothing, electronics, household goods
- Gifts: items sent to individuals or organizations
- Donations: items contributed to charities or non-profit organizations
- Samples: product samples for testing or evaluation

Unlike commercial goods, non-commercial cargo is often exempt from import/export duties. However, specific regulations and restrictions may still apply depending on the country and the nature of the goods.

items.question: What Documents Are Needed to Apply for a C/O (Certificate of Origin)?
items.answer:
- Commercial Invoice + Packing list
- Input raw material purchase invoices
- Manufacturing process
- Raw material consumption norm per product
- Local authority confirmation of product origin
- Trader profile/documents

items.question: What Are the Steps in the Fumigation Process for Export Goods?
items.answer:
Export cargo fumigation is a common measure using chemicals to treat insects and parasitic organisms on goods with paper/wood packaging, wooden pallets, or wooden crates. This method helps clean goods, prevents contamination during international transit, and limits the spread of microorganisms between countries.

Fumigation documents are issued after quarantine and medical disinfection authorities complete treatment with plant protection chemicals. Using fumigation certification not only protects cargo but also helps eliminate bacteria and termites in vessel compartments for sea transport. Bromide is the main chemical used in this process.

Goods that may require a fumigation certificate (subject to importer requirements).

The 3-step process includes:

- Step 1: Submit fumigation request
  - Fumigation location address
  - Execution time
  - Cargo type
  - Importing country
  - Cargo volume/weight

- Step 2: Carry out fumigation
  - Spray fumigation chemicals at port or designated location
  - Store cargo to allow treatment effectiveness

- Step 3: Complete fumigation process
  - Provide required information to issue the fumigation certificate
  - Review and confirm draft certificate
  - Receive certificate, make payment, and send certificate to customer

Fumigation plays an important role in protecting cargo and the supply chain, ensuring export transportation is safe and compliant with international requirements.

sea.heroSubtitle: LCL & FCL — Connecting Vietnam to Global Markets
sea.overviewP1: In the context of growing international trade, choosing a reliable shipping partner is a key factor in helping your business expand globally.
sea.overviewP2: With extensive experience and a global partner network, HACO Logistics is proud to deliver high-quality, flexible, and efficient sea freight services. We are committed to optimizing your supply chain, ensuring cargo is transported safely, on time, and at the most competitive cost.
sea.h1Desc: With our global partner network, we ensure flexible shipping routes connecting major seaports, helping your cargo reach its destination quickly and on schedule.
sea.h2Desc: Strict quality management processes and modern tracking systems keep you informed of your shipment status, giving you peace of mind about your assets' safety.
sea.h3Desc: Customized solutions and optimized processes help save costs and increase profitability for your business.
sea.h4Desc: From full container load (FCL), less than container load (LCL) to special and oversized shipments, we can handle everything professionally.
sea.ctaSubtitle: Contact us now for free consultation and the best quote for your shipment.

air.h3Desc: Connected to all international airports, shipping to every destination worldwide.
air.h4Desc: Cargo insurance, special goods handling, and absolute quality control.

customs.overviewP1: Customs declaration is a critical step in the import-export process. Accurate and timely declaration helps businesses avoid legal risks, minimize additional costs, and ensure swift customs clearance.
customs.overviewP2: With an experienced customs expert team, HACO Logistics provides comprehensive customs declaration services, from HS code classification, tax policy determination to completing customs clearance procedures. We are committed to providing absolute peace of mind for all your shipments.
customs.h1Desc: Electronic declaration via VNACCS/VCIS system, processing declarations in the shortest time, reducing customs clearance time.
customs.h2Desc: Correct HS code classification, accurate tax policy determination, avoiding additional costs and legal risks.
customs.h3Desc: Certified customs declarants with deep knowledge of import-export tax schedules and customs regulations.
customs.h4Desc: End-to-end support: document receipt, cargo classification, declaration, physical inspection, and cargo release.
customs.ctaSubtitle: Contact us now for free consultation and the fastest, most accurate customs support.
